task_name: Add JWT Authentication System to REST API

plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's a summary of the plan:

---

## Plan: JWT Authentication System

### New files (2)

**`src/middleware/auth.ts`**
- Exports `JWT_SECRET = "super-secret-key"` and `authenticate` middleware
- Reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, calls `jwt.verify`, attaches `req.user = { userId, username }` to the request
- Returns 401 if no token or invalid token
- Globally augments Express `Request` type so TypeScript strict mode accepts `req.user` in all route files

**`src/routes/auth.ts`**
- Password hashing via `crypto.createHash("sha256")` — no bcrypt, no new dependencies
- `POST /auth/register` → validates fields (400), rejects duplicate usernames (409), hashes & stores password, returns `{ token, user: { id, username, email } }` at 201
- `POST /auth/login` → finds user, compares hashed password, returns `{ token }` at 200 or 401

### Modified files (3)

**`src/routes/posts.ts`**
- Add `authenticate` as route-level middleware to POST, PUT, DELETE only
- POST: `userId` comes from `req.user!.userId` (not request body)
- PUT + DELETE: ownership check — `post.userId !== req.user!.userId` → 403 before any mutation

**`src/routes/comments.ts`**
- Add `authenticate` to POST `/posts/:postId/comments`, PUT `/comments/:id`, DELETE `/comments/:id`
- POST: `userId` from `req.user!.userId`; DELETE: ownership → 403 for non-owners

**`src/app.ts`**
- Import `authRouter` and mount at `app.use("/auth", authRouter)`

### Why existing tests are safe
`posts.test.ts` and `comments.test.ts` only test public GET endpoints — those routes receive zero changes and remain fully public.
